note: Tracks are overlapping level ranges; a learner progresses T0→T1→T2→T3→T4. Each track's entry points are root concepts (no prerequisites) within that track's domains.

tracks.name: Absolute Foundations
tracks.description: Entry-level mathematics. No prerequisites assumed. Covers logic, sets, number sense, and basic arithmetic.

tracks.name: Pre-University Mathematics
tracks.description: High-school to pre-university level. Builds on T0. Algebra, geometry, trigonometry, functions, sequences.

tracks.name: Undergraduate Core
tracks.description: Standard first/second-year university mathematics. Calculus, differential equations, linear algebra, probability, statistics, discrete mathematics.

tracks.name: Upper Undergraduate / Early Graduate
tracks.description: Third/fourth-year university and early MSc level. Abstract algebra, analysis, topology, numerical methods, optimization.

tracks.name: Graduate / Research Mathematics
tracks.description: MSc/PhD-level mathematics. Measure theory, functional analysis, category theory. Requires all prior tracks.
